subject: History
chapter: The Rise of Nationalism in Europe

activities_projects.title: Frederic Sorrieu’s Vision: A Deconstruction
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the utopian vs. realistic elements of 19th-century political art
materials: Print of 'The Dream of Worldwide Democratic and Social Republics'
steps: Deconstruct the symbols in Sorrieu's 1848 print (e.g., the shattered remains of absolutist institutions)
Identify which nations depicted were already nation-states and which were still aspirations in 1848
Write a critique on why Sorrieu categorized these groups by their distinct costumes and flags
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critical analysis of visual hagiography and political propaganda

activities_projects.title: Comparative Analysis: Bismarck vs. Cavour
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate different methodologies of 'Realpolitik' in unification
materials: Reference texts on Prussian and Piedmontese diplomacy
steps: Compare Bismarck’s 'Blood and Iron' with Cavour’s tactical diplomatic alliances with France
Analyze the role of the monarchy in both the German and Italian unification processes
Debate which leader faced greater internal ethnic/regional resistance
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency and historical evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Balkan Powder Keg: Mapping Conflict
activities_projects.objective: Understand the link between ethnic nationalism and the outbreak of WWI
materials: Historical Map of the Ottoman Empire (19th Century)
steps: Map the diverse ethnic groups within the Balkan region (Serbs, Bulgarians, Greeks, etc.)
Identify the overlapping territorial claims of the 'Big Powers' (Russia, Germany, England, Austro-Hungary)
Write an essay on how romantic nationalism led to the disintegration of the Ottoman Empire
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the complexity of ethnic nationalism and imperialism

activities_projects.title: The Napoleonic Code: A Legal Audit
activities_projects.objective: Assess the impact of administrative reforms on conquered territories
materials: Copy of the Civil Code of 1804 excerpts
steps: List the 'liberal' reforms (abolition of feudalism) vs. 'oppressive' measures (censorship, forced conscription)
Conduct a mock trial: Was Napoleon a 'liberator' or a 'modernizing tyrant' for Europe?
Create a flow chart showing how French rule stimulated 'anti-French' nationalism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Ability to handle historical contradictions and dialectics

activities_projects.title: The Frankfurt Parliament: A Post-Mortem
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the failure of liberal constitutionalism in 1848
materials: Research notes on St. Paul’s Church assembly
steps: Identify the social composition of the 831 elected representatives
Analyze why the parliament lost its social base (workers and artisans)
Explain the significance of Friedrich Wilhelm IV’s rejection of the crown
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the limitations of middle-class led revolutions
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of political failure factors (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Allegory Construction: Nation as Persona
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize the concept of 'Invented Traditions' in nationalism
steps: Study the attributes of Germania (Oak leaves = Heroism) and Marianne (Red Cap = Liberty)
Invent a contemporary allegory for a modern movement using at least 5 distinct symbols
Explain how personification helps in mass mobilization of an illiterate population
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the psychological tools of national identity construction
activities_projects.evaluation: Creative synthesis and symbolic depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Strange Case' of Britain: An Analytical Essay
materials: Case study documents on the Acts of Union (1707 & 1801)
steps: Trace the systematic suppression of Scottish and Irish cultures (Gaelic language, etc.)
Compare the 'unification' of Britain with the 'unification' of Germany
Analyze the role of the 'British Flag' and 'National Anthem' as tools of cultural hegemony
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between state-building and revolutionary nationalism

activities_projects.title: Guiseppe Mazzini: The Secret Society Blueprint
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of underground movements in political change
steps: Draft a 'Founding Charter' for a hypothetical secret society based on Mazzini’s principles
Analyze Metternich’s quote: 'The most dangerous enemy of our social order'
Evaluate the effectiveness of Mazzini’s 'God and People' philosophy in unifying Italy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the radical roots of 19th-century liberalism
activities_projects.evaluation: Authenticity of the charter draft (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historiography: The Greek War of Independence
activities_projects.objective: Examine the influence of 'Poets and Artists' on political events
steps: Research how the 'cradle of European civilization' narrative mobilized Western European support
Analyze the Treaty of Constantinople (1832) as a turning point
Present how 'Cultural Nationalism' precedes political sovereignty
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the role of culture in shaping geopolitical outcomes
activities_projects.evaluation: Integration of art and history (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Dual Nature of Nationalism: Imperialism Nexus
activities_projects.objective: Trace the evolution of nationalism into 19th-century imperialism
steps: Identify the point where democratic-liberal sentiments shifted toward aggressive expansion
Analyze how major powers exploited the nationalist aspirations of Balkan peoples
Create a presentation on how the 'ideals of 1848' were corrupted by 1914
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Advanced understanding of historical evolution and causality
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and synthesis of complex themes (10 marks)
